title: 5.x snapshot-lift decision
manager: A recorded decision — do 5.x-era serializations lift into v6-and-later, or get refused? — written down even if the answer is "refuse," because leaving it implicit is exactly how a silent corruption bug ships. This is one of two policy decisions this era owes: whether the oldest snapshots are worth carrying forward, or whether the clean break is safer, decided deliberately and on the record rather than by whatever the code happens to do.
cs: D9 decision: 5.x serializations lift-or-refuse, written down (omissions D9); either answer is acceptable, silence is not; interacts with version-gating (W6.93).
